_comment_general: Single source of truth for all pipeline parameters. Change values here, never hardcode in notebooks. This is what 'parameterised pipelines' means in the job spec.

api._comment_api: UK Police street level crime endpoint. No auth needed. Returns [] for months with no data yet (data lags ~2 months behind current date).

bristol._comment_points: The API takes a single lat/lng and returns crimes within a 1 mile radius. One point cannot cover all of Bristol, so we query a grid of points covering the city and deduplicate on the crime id afterwards. These 6 points cover central Bristol plus the main outer areas.

ingestion._comment_months: How many past months to backfill on first run. Police data is published roughly 2 months in arrears, so we start from 2 months ago and go back from there.

storage._comment_storage: Platform specific file paths. The notebooks detect whether they are running on Fabric or Databricks and pick the right block. Table names are shared: on Fabric they become Lakehouse tables, on Databricks they become Unity Catalog managed tables.

databricks._comment: Uses a Unity Catalog Volume. Create it once in the Catalog UI: catalog 'workspace', schema 'default', volume 'bristol'. Then upload this config file into it under a config/ folder.

data_quality._comment_dq: Thresholds the quality gate enforces. Tune these once you know your data volumes.
